text.title: langchain-production-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for langchain-production-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using RunnableParallel in a synchronous invoke() call expecting true parallel execution of multiple LLM branches.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sync RunnableParallel runs branches sequentially. For actual parallelism use ainvoke() or astream() with async branches.

failure-mode.description: Using hub.pull() in a production chain that may run in air-gapped or restricted network environments.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: hub.pull() fetches prompts from LangSmith Hub at runtime. If the Hub is down or the prompt is deleted, the chain fails with an unclear error. Inline prompts or locally committed templates are safer for production.

failure-mode.description: Importing from langchain-community without pinning the optional backend dependencies (pypdf, chromadb, etc.).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Community loaders depend on optional libraries not installed by default. Missing deps fail silently or raise confusing ImportError at invocation time, not at import time.
